content: Hepatic dosing adjusts drug regimens for liver impairment, which affects metabolism of hepatically-cleared drugs. Liver disease reduces CYP450 enzyme activity, decreases protein synthesis (lower albumin), and may cause portosystemic shunting bypassing first-pass metabolism. Child-Pugh score assesses severity: bilirubin, albumin, INR, ascites, encephalopathy (Class A: 5-6 points mild; B: 7-9 moderate; C: 10-15 severe). Drugs requiring hepatic adjustment: statins, benzodiazepines, opioids, warfarin, many antidepressants. Avoid hepatotoxic drugs in liver disease: acetaminophen >2g/day, isoniazid, methotrexate, amiodarone. Some drugs are safe due to renal elimination.
